seo.title: AutoGen Guardrails for Education
seo.description: Compliance guide for AutoGen agents in education. Meet FERPA, COPPA requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Education teams using AutoGen for AI tutoring and multi-agent learning systems face heightened risks due to strict privacy laws like FERPA and COPPA, which protect student data. Without compliance guardrails, infinite agent loops, unsafe code execution, or biased conclusions can expose sensitive data or deliver harmful content to minors. Vex provides essential filters to ensure safe, compliant deployments in educational environments.

complianceRequirements.description: Protects student education records from unauthorized disclosure, requiring parental consent for minors under 13.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex scans agent replies for PII detection and blocks conversations containing student IDs, names, or grades before transmission.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates verifiable parental consent for collecting personal information from children under 13.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces age-appropriate content filters and prevents data collection prompts in agent interactions with minors.

complianceRequirements.description: Vary by state but generally require encryption, access controls, and audit logs for student data.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex logs all agent decisions with compliance metadata and redacts sensitive data in multi-agent debates.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires educational content to be accessible to users with disabilities, including screen reader compatibility.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates agent-generated content for WCAG compliance and blocks inaccessible outputs like unlabelled images or complex tables.

industryRisks.name: Student Data Exposure
industryRisks.description: Agents inadvertently sharing names, grades, or IDs in conversation logs.
industryRisks.example: Tutor agent leaks student SSN during debugging session shared publicly.

industryRisks.name: Inappropriate Content to Minors
industryRisks.description: Multi-agent debates generating age-inappropriate or biased responses.
industryRisks.example: Agents role-playing historical violence without age filters during middle school history lesson.

industryRisks.name: Infinite Token Spending
industryRisks.description: Uncontrolled debates exhausting budgets while discussing non-educational topics.
industryRisks.example: Agents debating philosophy for 10k tokens instead of solving math problems.

industryRisks.name: Unsafe Code Execution
industryRisks.description: Student-submitted code executed without sandboxing, risking data breaches.
industryRisks.example: Malicious student code deletes database records during programming tutor session.

implementation.title: Register Vex as Reply Filter
implementation.description: Install and configure Vex to filter all agent replies for PII and compliance violations.
implementation.code:
import autogen
from vex_sdk import VexClient

# Install: pip install autogen vex-sdk
vex = VexClient(api_key="your-vex-key")

@vex.filter(guards=["pii-detection", "education-ferpa", "age-appropriate"])
def vex_reply_filter(message, sender, recipient):
    if vex.block(message):
        return "[COMPLIANCE BLOCKED] Response violates education policy."
    return message

config_list = [{"model": "gpt-4", "api_key": "openai-key"}]

llm_config = {"config_list": config_list, "reply_filter": [vex_reply_filter]}
student_proxy = autogen.UserProxyAgent(name="student", code_execution_config=False)
tutor = autogen.AssistantAgent(name="tutor", llm_config=llm_config)

student_proxy.initiate_chat(tutor, message="Help me with algebra homework")

implementation.title: Prevent Infinite Loops
implementation.description: Use Vex to detect and terminate circular conversations exceeding token limits.

implementation.title: Safe Code Execution for Students
implementation.description: Filter student code submissions to prevent malicious execution in programming tutors.

caseStudyScenario.heading: AutoGen in Education: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
Maplewood High School deployed AutoGen for personalized math tutoring across 2,000 students. The system featured three agents: a student interface proxy, a math problem generator, and a step-by-step tutor. During beta testing, teachers noticed agents entering infinite loops debating calculus proofs while students waited, burning through $500 in tokens weekly. Worse, the conversation logs contained student names and quiz scores, risking FERPA violations if shared externally.

A 12-year-old middle schooler prompted the system with 'show me cool science experiments,' triggering agents to generate instructions for chemical reactions inappropriate for unsupervised minors. The code execution agent ran student-submitted Python without sandboxing, allowing a prankster to write file-reading scripts that accessed teacher grading spreadsheets. Without compliance controls, the district faced potential lawsuits and accreditation reviews.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After integrating Vex as a reply filter, all PII was automatically redacted from logs, and loop detection terminated circular debates after 15 turns. Age-appropriate content filters blocked hazardous experiment instructions, redirecting to safe alternatives. The code guard sandboxed executions, preventing filesystem access while allowing legitimate programming practice. Token spending dropped 85%, and the district passed its FERPA audit with clean Vex compliance reports, enabling safe scale-up to 10,000 students.

cta.heading: Comply with Education Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Get started with Vex for AutoGen today to protect student data and ensure compliant AI tutoring.
